business.headline: Markets under pressure, rupee weakens. Are index funds a safer long-term bet?Sharp swings in equities, a weaker rupee and higher crude prices have pushed investors to reassess their strategy. Analysts say Nifty 50 index funds are drawing attention as a lower-cost long-term option, though volatility remains high.

What salary do you need to buy a home in Delhi-NCR now?A Rs 1 crore budget today may not fetch a decent home in many parts of Gurgaon or Delhi, while buyers earning even Rs 20 lakh annually are struggling to balance EMIs, savings, and lifestyle costs.

ITR filing for FY 2025–26 kicks off: Here's what you need to knowReady to file your ITR for FY 2025–26? The Income Tax Department has officially started this year's filing process by enabling ITR-1 and ITR-4 utilities. But what does this mean for taxpayers, and should you file early?

Why is it so difficult for NRIs to invest in India? Zerodha's Nithin Kamath explainsIndia wants more foreign capital, but could one of its biggest investor groups already be waiting at the door? Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath has raised questions over why NRIs still face so many hurdles while investing in India.

8th Pay Commission: When are revised salaries for govt employees likely to come?Speaking exclusively with IndiaToday.in, Dr Manjeet Singh Patel, National President of the All India NPS Employees Federation (AINPS), explained when central government employees could realistically expect revised salaries and pensions under the 8th Pay Commission.

Why has gold rally suddenly paused? Key reasons explainedJust days after gold prices surged sharply, the rally appears to have lost some steam. MCX gold saw only a small rise on Monday, signalling caution in the market. Here's a look at the key reasons why the yellow metal has suddenly slowed down.

Donald Trump made 3,700 stock trades in just 90 days. Wall Street is shockedFinancial disclosures linked to US President Donald Trump reveal more than 3,700 stock trades in just three months, triggering alarm on Wall Street over the scale, timing and potential conflicts involved.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The benchmark BSE Sensex plunged 939.10 points, or 1.25%, to 74,298.89 at 9:37 am, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 284 points, or 1.20%, to 23,359.50.

ICICI Prudential Life shares down 7% today: Why the stock is falling sharplyICICI Prudential Life shares dropped Rs 39.70, or 7.41%, to Rs 495.90 on the National Stock Exchange of India as of 9:32 am, making it one of the top losers on the benchmark indices.

Sensex opens over 800 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; Tata Steel down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 807.71 points to 74,430.28 at 9:18 am, while the Nifty 50 slipped 249.75 points to 23,393.75. Most of the broader market indices also slipped.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Markets are set for a weak open after a drone attack on a UAE nuclear plant pushed oil prices higher. Rising crude, a record-low rupee and mixed institutional flows are keeping sentiment cautious.

Will petrol and diesel prices rise again after Russian oil waiver ends?India may face fresh pressure on petrol and diesel prices after the US let a waiver on Russian oil purchases expire. With crude above USD 100 and West Asia tensions high, refiners and consumers could come under added strain.

Gautam Adani launches Rs 150 crore rural eye care initiative in BiharTogether, the Adani Centre for Eye (ACE) and Adani Training in Ophthalmic Medicine (ATOM) will create an ecosystem that will be capable of performing 3.3 lakh eye surgeries annually and training 1,000 eye care professionals every year.

The fine print behind unlimited health insurance policiesHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the first part of "The Unlimited Illusion", we examine what "unlimited" health insurance really means, how these policies actually work, and why the fine print could matter more than the headline promise.

CNG prices increased in Delhi-NCR by Re 1, second hike in 2 daysCNG will now cost Rs 80.09 per kg in Delhi, while consumers in Noida and Ghaziabad will pay Rs 88.70 per kg. The latest increase comes just two days after CNG prices were raised by Rs 2 per kg on Friday, resulting in an overall hike of Rs 3 per kg.

Delhi govt cuts VAT on jet fuel; move to benefit airlines, passengersDelhi has cut VAT on aviation turbine fuel from 25 per cent to 7 per cent after Maharashtra made a similar move. The step is aimed at easing pressure on airlines and passengers as global jet fuel prices climb.

Govt restricts silver bar imports, 99.9% purity category now needs permitThe Centre has restricted imports of certain silver bars, including 99.9% purity silver, with immediate effect amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

Selective compliance, systemic exclusion: How workers are left outIn the seventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, a factory inspection from 1988 reveals how workers, though recorded for contributions, remained excluded from statutory benefits through selective and incomplete compliance.

No Claim Bonus: The motor insurance reward many drivers lose unknowinglyHidden inside every motor insurance policy is something called No Claim Bonus, or NCB, a reward insurers give customers for not making claims. Over time, it can reduce premiums significantly. Yet despite being one of the most valuable benefits in motor insurance, it is also one of the least understood.

Inflation finds new fuelHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's latest fuel price hike could trigger a wider inflation ripple across the economy, raising concerns over costlier food, deliveries, transport and growing pressure on household budgets.

entertainment.headline: Karthik Subbaraj teams up with Ilaiyaraaja for milestone 10th film, shares video Director Karthik Subbaraj announced his first collaboration with Ilaiyaraaja for his 10th film, with the legendary composer also marking his 1540th project in a career spanning 50 years.

Welcome To The Jungle title track: OG vibes meet jungle chaos in Akshay's film Welcome To The Jungle's title track brings back the comic energy of the Welcome franchise with a nostalgic callback and a massive ensemble cast. The film is slated to release in theatres on June 26.

Chand Mera Dil gets U/A 16+ after censors removed 96 seconds of kissing scenes Lakshya and Ananya Panday's Chand Mera Dil has been cleared by the Central Board of Film Certification with a U/A 16+ certificate following certain cuts. The film will release in theatres on May 22.

Alia Bhatt returns to Mumbai after Cannes appearance, Ranbir Kapoor joins. Video Alia Bhatt returned to Mumbai with Ranbir Kapoor after attending the Cannes Film Festival 2026. Videos of the couple at the airport are now going viral on social media following Alia's glamorous red carpet appearances.

IU, Byeon Woo Seok apologise over Perfect Crown historical row IU and Byeon Woo Seok apologised after Perfect Crown drew criticism over Episode 11's coronation scene. The row has centred on historical symbols that viewers said undercut Korea's sovereign status.

Huma Qureshi says Aishwarya made Cannes important, recalls being inspired by her At the Cannes Film Festival 2026, actor Huma Qureshi expressed that Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's presence helped make global film festivals matter more to Indian actors and audiences. She said watching the Devdas actor on the red carpet made her want to do it too.

Why didn't Suriya mention Trisha? Karuppu gratitude note sparks fan debate Suriya's thank-you note for Karuppu sparked questions after Trisha Krishnan was not mentioned. The omission set off a fan debate, while others said the post focussed on technicians.

Truly mind boggling: Ishaan Khatter receives honour from Indian Army for Pippa Actor Ishaan Khatter was given honorary membership of 45 Cavalry for his portrayal of Captain Balram Singh Mehta in Pippa. The recognition, accompanied by a commemorative plaque, moved the actor, who called it the honour of a lifetime.

No alimony, no third party: Suraj Nambiar denies rumours after split with Mouni Roy Suraj Nambiar has reacted to claims linking a 'third party' to his separation from Mouni Roy. His response has brought fresh attention to speculation surrounding their split.

Raja Shivaji is Rs 100 crore strong, to topple Sairat's long-standing Marathi record Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji has crossed Rs 100 crore gross worldwide, becoming the second-highest grossing Marathi film ever after Sairat. The historical epic continues its strong theatrical run with steady collections in Maharashtra and the Hindi market.

Selena Gomez to star in The Brutalist director's X-rated film: Here's what we know Actor Selena Gomez is reportedly set to feature in an X-rated film helmed by The Brutalist director Brady Corbet, alongside Cate Blanchett and Michael Fassbender. The untitled project is drawing attention for its X-rated category, 65mm format and scale.

Kusha Kapila, Kubbra react as influencer Ankush Bahuguna faces homophobia in London Ankush Bahuguna shared a video showing homophobic remarks he faced on a London street while dressed in an all-pink outfit. The clip prompted strong support from actors and creators.

Anupam Kher: I want to remain a newcomer always Anupam Kher spoke to India Today about returning to theatre with his new play, Jaane Pehchaane Anjane. The actor also reflected on storytelling, live performances, and what continues to draw him back to the stage.

Jacob Elordi, Kendall Jenner step out with Kylie, Timothee after Hawaii trip. Pics Jacob Elordi and Kendall Jenner have once again sparked dating rumours after being seen on a late-night outing with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet in Los Angeles. The outing comes days after the pair were photographed vacationing together in Hawaii.

Aamir Khan says Thugs of Hindostan was 'basically Sholay', admits he made an error Aamir Khan said Thugs of Hindostan followed a Sholay-like format and changed heavily during casting. He said prioritising his character Firangi over the story was a mistake that hurt the film.

Such a shame: Ameesha Patel calls out online hate after Alia's Cannes appearance Actor Ameesha Patel posted a sharp message on social media after Alia Bhatt was trolled over her Cannes appearance. The actor wrote about the shift in attention to pattern of online negativity aimed at Indian actors.

Vir Das calls out flyer going barefoot mid-meal, internet takes sides Vir Das sparked an online debate after calling out a flyer for going barefoot and scratching his feet during a flight meal service. While many backed the comedian over hygiene concerns, others felt such incidents should be handled privately instead of online.

Soni Razdan reacts as trolls claim daughter Alia was ignored at Cannes Soni Razdan reacted after a viral Cannes clip of Alia Bhatt triggered online trolling. The row widened as a writer called the backlash a misread, gendered response driven by validation anxieties in a viral post on Instagram.

Pati Patni Aur...box office Day 3: Comedy-drama wraps 1st weekend with Rs 24 crore Pati Patni Aur Woh Do posed a stronger Sunday at the box office after a steady opening weekend. The film earned Rs 21 crore over the first weekend.

general news.headline: Suvendu Adhikari government ends Bengal's religion-based schemesThe West Bengal cabinet approved Rs 3,000 assistance for women, free bus travel, and changes to existing schemes. It said religion-based assistance programmes under two departments will continue this month before being phased out.

She took drugs: Noida's Twisha Sharma's in-laws in Bhopal reject harassment chargeTwisha Sharma's mother-in-law, Giribala Singh, has moved court for bail and alleged that the 31-year-old consumed drugs and died by suicide. Twisha's family is pressing for a fresh post-mortem and a wider probe.

Dear NTA, at least say sorry: A student writes for lakhs of NEET aspirantsIn a sharply worded letter to the National Testing Agency, an 18-year-old NEET 2026 aspirant recounts months of sacrifice and the shock of a re-NEET after reports of a paper leak, reflecting the anger and broken trust many candidates feel.

Trump's favourite Field Marshal asked China for nuclear submarines: ReportPakistan sought China's support for developing a sea-based nuclear second-strike capability during talks in 2024, signalling that Islamabad continued strategic negotiations with Beijing even as ties with Washington strengthened.

Americans turn Aamericans: Alphonso, Kesar from India fly off US supermarket shelvesFrom Seattle supermarkets to luxury mango subscription boxes, Indian mangoes are becoming a major summer craze in the US. The growing demand for Alphonso and Kesar mangoes from India comes even as exports remain limited and expensive.

Cockroach Janta Party debuts, admits two TMC MPs within daysThe latest political gimmick or party in India, whichever way you see it, is the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP). Coming right after Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant's cockroach remarks, the CJP is on a membership drive. Within two days, it has admitted two TMC MPs, Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad.

Umar Khalid case: Supreme Court says bail is rule even in UAPA, questions own orderThe Supreme Court noted on Monday that a binding precedent on bail under the UAPA, laid down in a 2021 ruling, was not properly applied while rejecting Umar Khalid's bail plea.

I'm trapped, can't talk: Noida woman, found dead in Bhopal, sent this last textTwisha Sharma's family has intensified its protest over her mysterious death in Bhopal, demanding a fresh AIIMS Delhi post-mortem and refusing last rites, while her final "I am trapped" message to a friend has added a new layer of intrigue to the case.

Why Vijay's alcohol double shot will resonate with his fansIn Tamil Nadu, alcohol is linked to more than just evening cheers. It spills into cinema, masculinity, and politics. CM C Joseph Vijay is shutting down TASMAC booze shops and enforcing a minimum age for alcohol access. This is why Vijay is confident that the moves won't backfire.

Tamil Nadu is in the family way again — the Santiago Martin family wayLottery King Santiago Martin has achieved what few business families can manage — becoming the quiet constant across India's political landscape. He worked it out pretty early that a family that whispers in every political ear — and lines all pockets — holds a leverage that no single dynasty can match.

Donald Trump made 3,700 stock trades in just 90 days. Wall Street is shockedFinancial disclosures linked to US President Donald Trump reveal more than 3,700 stock trades in just three months, triggering alarm on Wall Street over the scale, timing and potential conflicts involved.

Pilot was clutching controls: Witness reveals morgue horror after Air India crashRomin Vohra, who lost three family members in the Air India crash last June, told the Daily Mail that he witnessed scenes inside the hospital morgue that he "can never unsee".

Take me home, I'm in hell: Noida woman's text to mother 5 days before deathWhatsApp messages sent by Twisha Sharma to her mother and friend before her death have surfaced in the Bhopal case. The chats have sharpened her family's demand for a fresh post-mortem and a wider probe.

Delhi bakes at 45°C: IMD warns of severe heatwave conditions aheadThe latest spell marks one of the most widespread and severe heat episodes of the season so far, with dry continental winds and intense solar heating combining to rapidly push temperatures high.

8-hour CBI raid at house of man who bought leaked NEET paper for daughterDuring the raid at the accused's house in Maharashtra's Nanded, officials examined documents and questioned family members while tracing when the paper was obtained, who supplied it, and how the alleged Rs 5 lakh deal was arranged.

All will be forgiven: US orchestrated Imran Khan's ouster, leaked cable revealsA leaked Pakistani diplomatic cable has reignited controversy over the ouster of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, with investigative outlet Drop Site publishing the original document at the centre of the cricketer-turned-politician's claims of US-backed regime change.

Watch: Chaos inside IndiGo flight amid 25-minute blackout, no AC and lightsStranded in the sweltering heat, distressed passengers were seen desperately fanning themselves with whatever they could find, including files, newspapers, and sticks.

The fine print behind unlimited health insurance policiesHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the first part of "The Unlimited Illusion", we examine what "unlimited" health insurance really means, how these policies actually work, and why the fine print could matter more than the headline promise.

Lack of understanding: India rejects Dutch concerns over press freedom, minoritiesAddressing the media at The Hague, Ministry of External Affairs Secretary (West) Sibi George said criticism of India often stemmed from inadequate knowledge about the country's social and democratic fabric.

8th Pay Commission: When are revised salaries for govt employees likely to come?Speaking exclusively with IndiaToday.in, Dr Manjeet Singh Patel, National President of the All India NPS Employees Federation (AINPS), explained when central government employees could realistically expect revised salaries and pensions under the 8th Pay Commission.

Cannes cameras did not snub Alia Bhatt. The Indian internet didAlia Bhatt's Cannes red carpet appearance this year triggered online claims that photographers ignored her. The reaction shifted attention from the event to a wider fixation on foreign validation and Indian celebrity scrutiny.

Indian woman in Bangkok says fresh air, safety matter more than 10-minute deliveryShreya Mahendru said her move to Bangkok was shaped by a search for cleaner, quieter living. Her post sparked a wider debate on liveability, civic conditions and the value of everyday peace.

Woman dies by suicide, family claims harassment, demands for SUV, Rs 51 lakhThe family members of the victim alleged that the woman was continuously subjected to physical assault and mental harassment by her husband and in-laws for additional dowry.

Revanth Reddy's 2034 Delhi ambition is both a dare and a riskRevanth Reddy has publicly spelt out a 2034 plan that will keep him in charge of Telangana before a move to Delhi. The declaration projects stability and ambition, but also sharpens risks from rivals within the Congress.

sports.headline: Salah must be dropped from Liverpool farewell game for disrespecting Slot: Rooney Wayne Rooney has launched fierce criticism of Mohamed Salah, accusing the Liverpool forward of being "selfish" and "disrespectful" towards manager Arne Slot after his latest public remarks on the club's style of play.

BCCI not a public authority under RTI Act: Central Information Commission The Central Information Commission (CIC) on Monday ruled that the BCCI is not a "public authority" under the RTI Act, saying the cricket board is neither owned, controlled nor substantially financed by the government.

Dhoni's return not on for IPL 2026? Ashwin's post sends CSK fans in panic mode Ravichandran Ashwin's cryptic post has cast fresh doubt over MS Dhoni's return for Chennai Super Kings' home game against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The message has stirred anxiety among fans as Chennai juggle fitness concerns, team balance and playoff pressure.

MS Dhoni comeback talk dismissed by Gavaskar before must-win SRH game Former India captain Sunil Gavaskar said MS Dhoni is unlikely to return for Chennai Super Kings against Sunrisers Hyderabad, insisting that CSK's playoff push and Dhoni's incomplete recovery must take priority over sentiment.

Neymar slams 'nonsense' criticism: I have worked hard for FIFA World Cup Brazil's record scorer Neymar said he had to endure "nonsense" criticism as he worked his way back from serious injuries and is satisfied he has done all he can to prove he is fully fit and deserves a place in Carlo Ancelotti's World Cup squad.

Punjab Kings co-owner, Mohit Burman open to owning a WPL franchise Punjab Kings co-owner Mohit Burman says the franchise would love to own a WPL team in future, backing the rapid rise of women's cricket despite the BCCI ruling out immediate expansion.

Vaughan hits out at ECB over Archer's extended IPL stay before Lord's Test Former England captain Michael Vaughan has questioned the ECB's handling of Jofra Archer's availability ahead of England's first Test against New Zealand, saying central contracts must take precedence over IPL commitments.

KKR did not support me: Bangladesh's Litton Das makes bold claim on IPL stint Bangladesh star Litton Das has criticised Kolkata Knight Riders over a lack of communication and support during his brief IPL stint, saying he was informed of his debut only hours before the match.

RR fell 20 runs short after Starc's game-changing over, admits Rathour Rajasthan Royals assistant coach Vikram Rathour admitted that Mitchell Starc's decisive 15th over turned the match in Delhi Capitals' favour, conceding that RR fell at least 20 runs short and paid for poor batting decisions in their five-wicket defeat.

Preity Zinta's intense chat with Shreyas Iyer goes viral as PBKS lose six in a row Preity Zinta spoke at length with Shreyas Iyer after Punjab Kings lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Dharamsala. The exchange drew attention as Punjab slumped to a sixth straight defeat and RCB sealed a playoff place.

Who is Aaron Rai? Indian-origin golfer ends Britain's 100-year PGA title wait Aaron Rai, an Indian-origin golfer, surged past Alex Smalley and Jon Rahm to win the PGA Championship, becoming the first Englishman in 107 years to lift the title.

Virat Kohli deserves Glenn Phillips-level hype for athleticism, say fans after viral videoLivewire at 37, Virat Kohli left fans in awe during RCB's win over PBKS in Dharamshala. A viral clip of his electric fielding sparked massive social media reactions, with fans hailing the icon as the greatest athlete India has ever produced.

CSK vs SRH: Dhoni's potential farewell masks playoff drama in ChennaiChennai Super Kings face SunRisers Hyderabad in their final home game of the season. While a playoff spot might be at stake, the entire attention is set to be on the possibility that MS Dhoni might announce his retirement after the game.

Kotla feels like an away ground: Badani's brutal verdict on DC's home disadvantageDelhi Capitals coach Hemang Badani delivered a blunt assessment of his side's home struggles despite DC's crucial five-wicket win, admitting the Arun Jaitley Stadium no longer feels like a home venue after the team managed just two wins in seven matches in Delhi this IPL season.

IPL Play of the Day: Mitchell Starc comes clutch to rip the life out of RRIPL 2026, DC vs RR: Mitchell Starc rattled Rajasthan Royals with a sensational over in which he picked up three wickets, laying the foundation for Delhi Capitals' five-wicket win on Sunday at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

IPL chairman Arun Dhumal interview: Leagues over bilaterals, real life over reelsIn an exclusive conversation at the HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala, league chairman Arun Singh Dhumal is candid about the IPL's billion-dollar future, the mid-season discipline storm, and why he thinks social media is the most anti-social thing ever invented.

Elina Svitolina wins Italian Open, Gael Monfils bursts with pride: What a womanElina Svitolina defeated Coco Gauff to win the 2026 Italian Open title in Rome, completing an emotional comeback story as Gael Monfils paid a heartfelt tribute to his wife. The Ukrainian secured her first WTA 1000 title since giving birth to her daughter in 2022.

Jose Mourinho breaks silence on Real Madrid rumoursBenfica head coach Jose Mourinho has clarified rumours of him joining Real Madrid in the upcoming season. Mourinho has said that there has been no direct approach made to him by the club in the recent weeks.

Riyan Parag furious after RR's loss to DC: Shouldn't be in race for IPL playoffsRajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was not happy with the team after sloppy mistakes in the field led to the team's loss against Delhi Capitals. Parag said that if those are the team's standards, they do not deserve to be in contention for the playoff spots.

Delhi Capitals do PBKS a favour with commanding win over RajasthanIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals outclassed Rajasthan Royals by 5-wicket at the Arun Jaitley Stadium to keep their slim play-off hopes alive. Mitchell Starc was the star performer for the Capitals with a fiery spell that dismantled RR's middle order.

MotoGP: Horrible crash in Spain sends Alex Marquez into the wallA horrifying high-speed crash involving Alex Marquez brought the Catalunya Grand Prix to a halt on Sunday after the Spanish rider slammed into Pedro Acosta and crashed violently into the barriers. Marquez suffered multiple injuries, while his motorcycle was left completely destroyed in the incident.

Jannik Sinner clinches Italian Open title, becomes youngest to Career Golden MastersJannik Sinner defeated Casper Ruud 6-4, 6-4 to win the 2026 Italian Open, becoming the youngest player to complete the Career Golden Masters by winning all nine ATP Masters titles. The victory also saw him make history as the first man to win the first five Masters events in a single season.

Rishabh Pant likely to lose leadership role in Test team amid IPL failuresTwo terrible seasons with Lucknow Super Giants has put questions on Rishabh Pant's leadership credentials. Rumours suggest that BCCI top brass are considering removing him from the vice-captain's position in Test cricket.

Could IPL playoffs be played behind closed doors amid PM Modi's appeal?IPL 2026: Concerns have emerged over the scheduling and logistics of a major sporting event in India amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and calls for austerity measures. Organisers said they will follow any directives issued by the Government of India regarding potential changes.

Farewell on IPL return? MS Dhoni trains before CSK vs SRH, sparks buzzIPL 2026: MS Dhoni is set to be at the centre of massive anticipation as CSK prepare for their final home game of IPL 2026 against SRH at Chepauk, with speculation growing over a possible appearance after a calf injury ruled him out of all matches so far.

Bruno Fernandes equals Premier League record as Man Utd win VAR-marred clashBruno Fernandes matched the Premier League assist record as Manchester United beat Nottingham Forest 3-2 at Old Trafford. The win kept United third, but a prolonged VAR handball call dominated the fallout.

Punjab's IPL season did not derail due to off-field controversies: Bowling coachPunjab Kings are on the brink of being eliminated from the IPL 2026 playoffs race. Punjab have endured a difficult time on and off the field, resulting in a horror performance in the second half this season.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL record books with six-hitting featIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hit his 43rd six of the season during Rajasthan Royals' match against Delhi Capitals. At 15, he has reset the Indian benchmark and moved within sight of Chris Gayle's overall IPL record.

Punjab Kings in freefall after 6 defeats in a row. Can they still reach playoffs?IPL 2026, PBKS vs RCB: Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday, May 17 at the HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala and are now in serious trouble as far as qualifying for the playoffs is concerned.

RCB storm into playoffs as Punjab Kings complete beyond-believable fall in HimachalIPL 2026, PBKS vs RCB: Punjab Kings suffered their sixth straight defeat as the Himachal escape offered them no relief. Fifties from Virat Kohli and Venkatesh Iyer propelled RCB into the playoffs after the tourists sealed a 23-run win in Dharamsala on Sunday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
